claimText: The Globally Important Agricultural Heritage Systems (GIAHS) Programme is operated by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO).
evidenceExcerpt: GIAHS is a programme operated by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO).
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the nodal international organization responsible for the GIAHS programme.

claimText: The traditional agroforestry system for the erva-mate plant in Brazil is recognized as a Globally Important Agricultural Heritage System (GIAHS).
evidenceExcerpt: Six new sites were added to the FAO's GIAHS Programme, including a traditional agroforestry system for the erva-mate plant in Brazil
examUse: Correct-pair question matching traditional agricultural systems to their respective countries.

claimText: Specialised farming systems for pearl mussels, white tea, and pears in China are recognized as Globally Important Agricultural Heritage Systems (GIAHS).
evidenceExcerpt: specialised farming systems in China (pearl mussels, white tea, pears)
examUse: Correct-pair question matching agricultural heritage systems to their respective countries.
